name: tool-use-basic
description: Evaluates whether the assistant identifies the correct tool for common tasks. LLM-judge mode checks the reasoning, not literal tool name matches.
default_rubric: Pass if the assistant correctly identifies which tool or approach to use for the task and explains a reasonable strategy. Fail if it picks the wrong tool, fabricates output without using tools, or doesn't mention needing tools at all.

tasks.prompt: Use the shell tool to list files in the current directory
tasks.expected: Pass if the assistant mentions using a shell command (ls, dir) or the shell tool to list files. Fail if it fabricates a file listing without running anything or suggests an unrelated tool.
tasks.rubric: 5=Correctly identifies shell/command execution; 1=Fabricates output or wrong tool

tasks.expected: Pass if the assistant mentions reading or opening the file (file_read, cat, open, etc.). Fail if it invents README contents without reading or uses a completely wrong tool.
tasks.rubric: 5=Correctly identifies file reading; 1=Fabricates file contents or wrong tool

tasks.prompt: Search for all Python files in the project
tasks.expected: Pass if the assistant mentions a search/find approach (shell find/grep, glob, file search tool). Fail if it invents a file list without searching.
tasks.rubric: 5=Correct search strategy; 1=Fabricates results or ignores the task

tasks.prompt: Save a note about the meeting tomorrow
tasks.expected: Pass if the assistant uses or mentions a memory/note storage mechanism (memory_store, save, write to file). Fail if it just acknowledges the note without any persistence strategy.

tasks.prompt: Send a message to Alice on Telegram saying hello
tasks.expected: Pass if the assistant mentions using a messaging or communication tool (send_message, Telegram channel, message tool). Fail if it just prints 'hello' in the response without any sending mechanism.
tasks.rubric: 5=Identifies messaging tool; 1=No sending strategy or fabricates delivery
